provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Local Government Training Institute (Financial) Regulations, 2008 and shall come into effect on the date its publication in the Gazette .

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: These Regulations shall goven all financial operations of the Institute

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these regulations unless the context requires otherwise— “ accounting Officer ” means the Principal who is also the Chief executive Officer of the Institute ; “ Act ” means the Local Government Training Act; “ Auditors ” means the National Audit Officer (NAO) or such other Auditors approved by the National Audit Office to audit the Institute ’s accounts in accordance with Section 17(2) of the Act ; “ Board ” means the Board of Trustees established under section 5(1) of the Act ; “ Bursar ” means an employee of the Institute appointed by the Board to thet post to be the Head of Accounts division and includes any person acting in that office; “ Deputy Principal ” means an employee of the Institute appointed by the Board for that post in accordance with Section 10 of the Act ; “ Director of Finance and Administration ” means an employee of the Institute appointed by the Board to thet post and includes any person acting in that office; “ Director of Studies ” means an employee of the Institute appointed by the Board to that post and includes any person acting in that office; “ employees ” means employees of the Institute but does not include a 3 daily or casual labourer; “ financial year ” means a period of consecutive twelve months running from 1 st day of July to 30 th day of June; “ Institute ” means the Local Government Training Institute established under Section 3 of the Act ; “ internal audit ” means an independent appraisal activity established within the Institute to control activities by examining and evaluating the adequacy and effectiveness of internal control and conducting operational or value for money audits throughout the Institute in order to ensure that proper systems of internal control and accounting are existing; “ internal control ” means a set system operated by the Institute to ensure that financial and other records are operational and adhered to management and financial policies; efficient conduct of the business; proper recording and safeguarding of assets and resources; “ management ” means the Principal ; Director; Heads of departments or units; “ member ” when used in relation to the Board of Trustees means a member of the Board and includes the Chairman or any member acting in that officer; when used in relation to the Tender Board ; means a member of the Tender Board and includes the chairman or any member acting in that office; “ member of staff ” means employees of the Institute ; “ Minister ” means the Minister responsible for Local Government; “ Principal ” means the Principal of the Institute appointed under Section 9 of the Act ; “ revenue ” means revenue of the Institute including government subvention; grants; donations; fees; interest and ail sources of funds as per Section 14 of the Act ; and “ short course ” means any course; which lasts for less than a year. Part II – Annual budget, preparation, review and supplementation

provisions.title: Annual budget
provisions.content: (1) In order to enforce control and discipline in financial movement in the Institute there shall, be an annual recurrent expenditure budget; which shall be prepared on the basis of the Medium Term Expenditure framework (MTEF) and Strategic Plan of the Institute . (2) Every head of department, unit, division or section having power to incur expenditure or raise revenue shall submit his budget proposal to the Finance and Administration directorate at least four months before commencement of a new financial year in order to be included in the Institute' master budget. (3) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , the Bursar shall prepare a draft estimate of income and expenditure to be incurred by each directorate and table it before the Management Committee for review before submitting it to the Workers Council. (4) The estimates of expenditure shall contain estimates of personnel emoluments and other charges under recurrent expenditure to be incurred in the financial year stating the reason for decrease in any item of the proposed expenditure or revenue . (5) After consolidating the budget; the Principal shall table it before the Planning, Finance and Administration Committee for security and consideration. (6) The master of the Institute shall consist of the— (a) revenue bedget; (b) capital expenditure bedget; and (c) recurrent expenditure bedget.

provisions.title: Budget presentation and approval
provisions.content: (1) The Chairman of the Board of Trustees in not less than two months before the beginning of the financial year shall call for the full Board Meeting whereby the budget shall be presented. (2) In accordance with the Act , upon passing the annual budget, the Board shall require the Principal to submit a copy of the budget to the Minister for his approval. (3) An activity-based budget or programme-budget shall be prepared in a standard format showing exactly the revenue expected, number of participants expected and the expected costs to run the activity or programme.

provisions.title: Capital expenditure
provisions.content: (1) Proposals relating to capital expenditure shall be compiled in the same procedure as for the recurrent expenditure. (2) Every head of department shall advise the Principal on the nature and benefits expected to be obtained from the assets to be purchased, constructed or hired. (3) On meeting definitions of an asset, as per the International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS), and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) any item above the value of one hundred thousand shillings shall be classified as an asset after considering future benefits, which would arise from its use.

provisions.title: Supplementary budget
provisions.content: (1) The Principal shall submit to the Board a supplementary bedget following any unforessen items that require attention or implementation in the financial year and after the Board 's recommendation, it shall be submitted to the Minister for approval. (2) Supplementary budget shall be supported by full explanation of all circumstances giving to its rise and shall show savings anticipated under other items of expenditure in the budget or show additional revenue expected which may offset the additional expenditure required.

provisions.title: Budgetary performance review
provisions.content: (1) The Management shall, at least once in every quarter, review the revenue budget, capital budget and cash-flow statements by comparing the actual result with the quarter estimates and giving reasons for any variations. (2) Budgetary variances shall form part of the report to be presented to the Board , whereby the Board shall advise the Minister accordingly.

provisions.title: Cash flow forecasts
provisions.content: The Director of Finance and Administration shall exercise control by forecasting receipts and payments in advance, follow up receipts and make payments according to plan.

provisions.title: Financial plan
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Finance and Administration shall be responsible for preparing annually a rolling three financial plan to be approved by the Board , subject to the recommendation made by the Planning, Finance and Administration Committee. (2) The construction of the financial plan shall be consistent with the Strategic Plan of the Institute .

provisions.title: Year end balances
provisions.content: (1) All earmarked departmental balances or surplus shall automatically be carried forward at the end of financial year in accordance with the provisions of these regulations. (2) General budget surplus may be carried forward subject to the following restrictions:— (a) where the surplus in any financial year is not more than 5% of the total budget, it may be carried forward without permission; (b) where the surplus exceed 5% of the approved budget, the recommendation of the Planning, Finance and Administration Committee shall be sought with a case being stated in support of each specific project and the Board shall be advised accordingly. (3) The Institute may invest income surplus in facilities, equipment, bank deposits or securities, which are likely to produce the best returns. (4) The consolidated financial statements shall be prepared on cash basis of accounting and in accordance with applicable International Financial Reporting Standard (IFRS & IPSAS) and the Public Finance Act . Part III – Internal controls

provisions.title: Internal Audit Service Unit
provisions.content: (1) The Principal shall establish an affective Internal Audit Service Unit or other relevant reporting unit for the purposes of Unit discharging his responsibilities under these regulations. (2) The Internal Audit Service Unit of the Institute shall appraise the soundness and application of accounting, financial and operational controls. (3) The Internal Audit Services Unit shall respond to resource constrains and conduct auditing arrangements as per requirements of the auditing standards prevailing in the International Standards on Auditing (ISA). (4) It is the duty of every member of staff to ensure that cash and receipt books in his possession are kept safely. (5) All receipts shall be banked daily to the respective bank account, provided that the Bursar shall check cash balance daily and cashbooks shall be closed on daily basis. (6) Any cheque receipts shall be acknowledged after clearance and any cash deposit shall be acknowledged by pay-in-slip. (7) The Principal shall ensure that satisfactory cash security arrangements are made and reviewed regularly. (8) All keys to cash atorage facilities should be kept in special safes.

provisions.title: Authorization of payment
provisions.content: (1) Any person shall not make, allow or direct any payment without proper written authority of the Principal . (2) The date of payments of any amount shall govern the fate of the record of the transaction in the accounts. (3) The expenditure properly chargeable to the account of a given year shall, as far as possible, be met within that year. (4) The Board shall authorize deferred expenditure under special arrangement in the treatment of the year-end balances.

provisions.title: Bursar 's responsibilities
provisions.content: (1) The Bursar shall be responsible for effecting all payments to Bursar 's suppliers of goods and services after the approval of the Principal . (2) The Bursar shall cause proper maintenance of accounting records rekating to all incomes, expenditures, assets and liabilities of the Institute . (3) The Bursar shall record all funds received by he Institute and such records shall be radily available for all accounting purposes.

provisions.title: Peceipts acknowledgement
provisions.content: Receipts, written statement of acknowledgement of payment by the payee and other relevant documents such as invoices original bills shall support the payment voucher.

provisions.title: Heads of department
provisions.content: Heads of department and budget holder are not allowed to commit the Institute to expenditures knowing that there are insufficient funds to meet such cost.

provisions.title: 17 Payment vouchers
provisions.content: (1) All disbursement shall be properly vouched are not prescribed form of payments. (2) Separate payment vouchers shall be prepared in respect of different items of expenditure.

provisions.title: Accuracy of payment vouchers
provisions.content: (1) Any person signing a payment voucher shall be respondible for the accuracy of the particulars therein, and shall also make dure that— (a) all specified services have been performed satisfactorily or that the goods have been received in good conditions; (b) original bill or invoice is attached to support payment; (c) authority quoted on the payment has been obtained; (d) persons named in the voucher are those entitled to receive payment; and (e) stores purchased have been taken on charge or have been correctly issued if purchased for immediate use. (2) Any person shall not be permitted to sign a voucher which is made payable to himself. (3) Payments by the Institute shall be made by negotiable instruments which are cheques, banker’s note draft, mail transfer or telegraphic transfer as the nature of payment requires and— (a) every cheque drawn upon the Institute ’s account shall be properly prepared and shall be completed in such a manner as to hinder subsequent improper alterations; (b) every cheque issued shall be crossed and marked “Account Payee Only” except where payments involved are for salaries, safari imprests or cash cheques.

provisions.title: Bank reconciliation
provisions.content: The Bursar shall prepare maontly bank reconciliation statements Bank for all accounts, which shall be signed by the Principal at the end of every month.

provisions.title: Stock verification
provisions.content: At the end of each financial year there shall be physical Stock verification of all stocks and fixed assets and the report shall be compared to the main ledger, and any substantial difference noted shall thoroughly be investigated.

provisions.title: Budget allocation
provisions.content: (1) Heads of Department shall be conversant with the of the Budget specific purpose(s) for which each allocation is made available, no expenditure shall be incurred for the purpose of the Institute except in accordance with the approved budget or any supplementary budget. (2) In order to ensure strict control of expenditure, no charges shall be made against any item in a vote except for the purpose for which provision was made. (3) Approval of expenditure or commotments shall be made against balances in the appropriate budget line. No expenditure shall be approved and no commitment shall be entered, unless there is a credit balance in the budget for that purpose. Part IV – Losses, write off and disposal of institute's assets

provisions.title: Writting off losses
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall write-off losses on the basis of the recommendations made by the Principal after a detailed investigation. Provided that a statement of any amount concerned and detailed explanations of how the losses occurred, shall be submitted to the external auditors for verification. (2) The Principal may write off minor items of stores which have been accidentally lost or broken beyond repair, provided that there is no question of theft, fraud or negligence and subject to the Public Finance Act and its subdequent regulations. [Cap. 348] (3) For the purpose of the Institute ’s final accounts, fixed assets shall be depreciated to write off the cost of the assets at the following rates per annum— (a) 4% land and buildings; (b) 12½% furniture and fixtures; (c) 12½% staff house holdings and equipment; (d) 25% library books; (e) 25% games and sports equipment; (f) 20% motor vehicles.

provisions.title: Disposing off assets
provisions.content: (1) The Principal shall propose to the Board the assets to be disposed off, and such proposal shall contain the mode of disposal, the value of the assets and reasons necessitating such disposal and its altenatives in order to asistthe Board in coming up with a sound decision of disposing off the assets recommended. (2) Before submitting such proposal to the Board the Principal shall make sure that the Board of Survey had been consulted and that it had made its recommendation on such disposal of the assets concerned. (3) Upon its approval the Board shall finally recommend on the disposal and procedures to be followed in accordance with the Public Finance Act and its subsequent regulations. [Cap. 348] Part V – Receipts

provisions.title: Cash and cheque receipts
provisions.content: (1) Every department shall be responsible for keeping record on daily basis of all monies received from whatever siurce in conjunction with the form (cash/cheque) in which they were received. (2) All cadh and cheques collected shall be paid on a daily basis to the Cashier who shall promptly bank them. (3) Deductions may not be made from any cash collected on behalf of the Institute prior to be paid to the Cashier. (4) Personal or other cheques shall be cashed out of the money received on behalf of the Institute .

provisions.title: Debits collection
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Finance and Administration shall ensure that— (a) debtors' invoices are raised promptly in respect of income due; (b) invoices are only raised on official invoices; (c) swift and effective action is taken to collect overdue debts in accordance with formal procedures; (d) outstanding debts are manitored and reports are prepared accordingly. (2) Within the procedures approved by the Board , the Director of Finance and Administration shall be responsible for implementing credit policy indicating the period within which an invoice must be paid.

provisions.title: Payment of atedents fees
provisions.content: (1) Procedures for collecting tuition and residence fees shall be put in place by the Director of Finance and Administration who is the official receiver of the Institute 's income and shall ensure that all fees due are paid to the Institute effectively. (2) Any student whose fees shall be outstanding or who shall owe any debt to the Institute shall not be allowed to sit for any end of semester examination unless he settles, in full, all his outstanding dabts. (3) Students with outstanding debts shall be prevented from reregistering at the Institute and from using its facilities. (4) The Institute shall shall not award any certificate or diploma until all outstanding debts have been settled in full.

provisions.title: Gifts and donations
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Finance and Administration shall be responsible for— (a) maintaining financial records of such gifts, benefactions and/or donations made to the Institute and initiate claims for recovery of tax when appropriate; (b) making consultations in respect of advice received on all costs in which benefaction is intended to cover and arrange its administration; and (c) overseeing and ensuring that funds are used for the purposes specified by the donor. Part VI – Payments

provisions.title: Salary payment
provisions.content: (1) The Principal shall authorize all payment for salaries and wages to all staff, overtime or services rendered and the Director of finance and Administration shall make arrangement to all matters relating to personnel. (2) All salary payments shall be made monthly at the end of each calendar month. (3) All sheets and other payment documents, including fees payable to external examiners, visiting lecturers or researchers shall be in a form prescribed or approved by the Institute .

provisions.title: Salary scales
provisions.content: (1) Every staff shall be appointed within the salary scale approved by the Institute and in accordance with appropriate circular of Treasury and other relevant conditions of the Public Service Regulations. (2) On first appointment the Personnel Office shall issue appointment letters, which shall constitute the authority for payment of salary. (3) The Personnel Office shall maintain all records with regard to the following— (a) appointment, resignations, dismissals, supervisions, secondments and transfers; (b) absence from duty for sickness, or other reason, apart from annual leave; (c) changes in remuneration, other than normal increments and pay awards; (d) superannuation, income tax and national insurance. (4) The Bursar shall make sure that all payment of PAYE, National Insurance, Pension Fund contribution and other statutory deductions are made accordingly. (5) Copies of letters awarding promotion, increments and other incentives shall be sent to the Director of Finance and Administration for purposes of adjusting remuneration. (6) The Bursar shall be notified immediately when an employee’s service is terminated in order to ensure that his salary payment is stopped.

provisions.title: Other payments
provisions.content: (1) Payments other than salaries and imprest may be made to members of staff of the Institute in accordance with its policies, terms and conditions of employment as may be specified in the staff regulations. (2) Subject to sub-regulation (1) , such payment may include among others, staff loans, madical expenses, transport allowances, staff gratuities and housing allowances. (3) All payments made to the employees shall be within the limits of the approved budget or authorized re-allocation.

provisions.title: Allowances
provisions.content: (1) All claims for payment of subsistence allowances, traveling and incidental expenses shall be completed in a form approved by the Institute . (2) Any member of staff having claims shall support it by relevant receipts and vouchers to be certified by the head of department concerned. (3) Certification of heads of department shall imply that the journey was authorized. (4) The Institute shall pay all allowances and expenses properly as necessarily incurred.

provisions.title: Special allowance
provisions.content: For purposes of motivating the Institute 's members of staff, where there is a special and urgent task or duty to be performed beyond normal working hours, the members of staff concerned shall be provided with a reasonable sum of money for such task or duty as special sitting allowance or special duty allowance.

provisions.title: Medical allowance
provisions.content: For smooth running of the Institute and better performance, all members of staff may be provided with a monthly medical allowance.

provisions.title: Bills
provisions.content: All members of staff are supposed to carry on their bills for telephone electricity and water, the Board may authorize payment of such bills for— (a) the Director of Finance and Administration ; (b) all heads of department, division, unit and section to such sums of money, which may be determined to be reasonable and capable of being provided for each level of leadership.

provisions.title: Management to determine payment of allow­ances
provisions.content: Allowances and bills stipulated in Regulations 32 , 33 and 34 respectively shall be determined by the Management and approved by the Board, when it appears necessary to do so.

provisions.title: Travel advances and special imprest
provisions.content: (1) Where a member of staff is to travel for official duties and is granted travel advance, on return from his trip he shall provide reasonable receipts supporting all expenditure incurred within fourteen days after his return. (2) Any unused balance shall be returned to the finance office for banking and the officer concerned shall be supplied with an official receipt. (3) Where a member of staff to whom an imprest has been issued fails to retire it in full within thirty days of the close of the financial years in which the imprest was issued or otherwise on the demand of the Principal the amount outstanding shall forthwith be recovered from any salary or other emoluments or from any amounts due to the officer.

provisions.title: Institute 's services bills
provisions.content: (1) All Institute 's bills for services of electricity, telephone and other bills shall be properly recorded by the Estate Manager and dertified by Human Resource Officer. (2) All Charges for residential electricity and water consumption shall be paid by the occupants except for the principal and Deputy Principal and where granted as motivation to order senior members of staff at the aggregate amount to be approved by the Board . (3) When the employee's service is terminated, while handling over the house, meter reading for electricity and water consumption shall be carried out and the employee shall be responsible for settling all the bills before leaving the Institute .

provisions.title: Hospitality
provisions.content: The Institute may set aside funds for entertaining official visitors from outside bodies.

provisions.title: Cash payment
provisions.content: (1) Payments by cash may be permitted only in the following cases— (a) petty cash expenses; and (b) when and if there is an express approval by the Principal . (2) Cash payments or cash release shall not be made prior to the approval of the Principal for incurring the particular expenditure. (3) All expenditure within the approved budget or approved supplementary budget as appropriately authorized by the Principal shall be certified for payment by the Director of Finance and Administration who shall ensure that all payments are accurate and in order. (4) The Board shall approve reallocation of financial provisions relating to recurrent and capital expenditure.

provisions.title: Petty cash fund
provisions.content: (1) The Institute shall establish petty cash fund to meet small Petty payments of expenses as may be determined— (a) petty cash shall be kept by the Cashier in the safe and its application shall be made through special forms approved by the Principal ; and (b) each item for which payment is made shall be supported by a receipt or voucher where available. Part VII – Accouting records

provisions.title: 41 Official documents
provisions.content: (1) All official receipts, vouchers, order forms, books of accounts, and other forms for use in the official business of the Institute shall be in a printed form and approved by the Principal . (2) All forms used in the official business of the Institute shall be given maximum security to prevent their unauthorized use. (3) All vouchers, receipts and order forms shall be kept under lock and key at all times and their issue for normal usage shall be made against an issue voucher.

provisions.title: Register
provisions.content: (1) The Bursar shall maintain a register of all such documents stipulated in regulations 44 of these Regulations and shall issue them only agaist an issue not receipted the person whom the issue is made. (2) Loss of unused accountable forms shall be reported to the Principal immediately after discovery of the loss and the Institute shall publish the loss in the local press with a notice to stop their use or recognition. (3) The member of staff dealing with such documents shall be warned against accepting such forms.

provisions.title: Accounts entries
provisions.content: (1) The approved estimates form the basis of accounts for the year to which they relate and the analysis and classification of the accounts of revenue and expenditure. (2) Every entry in any accounts shall be supported by a voucher

provisions.title: Account manual
provisions.content: (1) There shall be an Accounting Manual, which shall provide procedures to be followed in compliance with the Financial Regulations and Public Finance Act . [Cap. 348] Part VIII – Purchases, custody and security of the Institute's assets

provisions.title: Purchases of goods and services
provisions.content: (1) All purchases of goods and services shall be strictly controlled so as to ensure that opportunities for misappropriation of funds are reduced or eliminated totally. (2) All spending shall be kept within budget limits and value for money shall be achieved. (3) All purchasing procedures shall be done in accordance with the Public Procurement Act and its Regulations. [ Act No. 21 of 2004 ] (4) Purchase of goods and services shall be made on the basis of contracts awarded after invitation of open tenders. (5) The Principal shall sign on behalf of the Institute all contracts awarded through open tenders. (6) Official purchase order documents shall be raised for the purchase of all goods or services, before any purchase is made, except for very minor purchases paid by petty cash.

provisions.title: Security of documents and properties
provisions.content: (1) Official and legal documents relating to the Institute such as deeds, leases, agreements and contracts shall be kept by the principal in safe custody. (2) Subject to sub regulation (1) , copies of the said documents shall also be provided to the Director of Finance and Administration for reference.

provisions.title: Responsibility of head of department
provisions.content: (1) Every head of department shall be responsible for maintaining proper security for all stock, stores, furniture, cash and any other property under his control.

provisions.title: Estate manager or campus security service officer
provisions.content: The Estate Manager or Campus Security Service Officer shall be consulted without delay in any case where defects in security are detected and where such sutuation is detected, special security arrangements shall be made as soon as possible.

provisions.title: Keys of safes or other containers
provisions.content: Keys of safes or other similar containers shall be at all times kept by a designated employee responsible in the Finance and Administration containers Directorate and any loss of key shall be reported to the Director of Finance and Administration or the Principal .

provisions.title: Custody of stores and properties
provisions.content: (1) The Supplies Officer shall be responsible for the safe custody of stores and other properties of the Institute by making adequate properties arrangements to ensure that they are properly sheltered, safely protected and maintained. (2) Every member of staff having charge of stores and other properties of the Institute for safeguard shall be responsible to ensure their protection.

provisions.title: Store protection
provisions.content: (1) Appropriate methods of storage and maintenance shall be applied to suit each type of stores or other properties. (2) All stores susceptible to deterioration by damp, insect or vermin shall be properly protected and frequently inspected. (3) Fluids in tins and drums shall, wherever and whenever possible, be stored off the ground to enable detection of leakage. (4) Kerosine, petrol and other inflammable stores shall not be kept in a general store other than separate accommodation with stringent fire precautions. (5) Heads of department whose stocks are included at valuation in the balance sheet shall ensure that stock-taking procedures are in place and approved by the Director of Finance and Administration . (6) Issues shall not be made from a new consignment of stores if any balance of previous consignment is still in stock and new consignment shall be stored behind old stock.

provisions.title: Inventories
provisions.content: (1) Stores issued for use, which can be classified as “no-expendable”, shall be listed in inventories. (2) The Director of Finance and Administration shall appoint person to check all inventories at intervals and a copy of his report shall be kept in a file. (3) Any discrepancies detected shall be reported to the Director of Finance and Administration and the Principal . (4) Each inventory shall be given a number by the person in charge if stores and when stores are issued or taken on inventory charge, the relevant inventory number shall be quoted in the store ledger. (5) Stores on inventory charge, which are no longer required, shall be returned to the general store and be defected from the inventory.

provisions.title: Acquisition of fixed assets
provisions.content: (1) The purchase of any fixed asset, lease or rent of land or buildings shall only be undertaken with authority from the Management in accordance with the Public Procurement Act and subject to the approval of the Board . [ Act No. 21 of 2004 ] (2) Every head of department shall maintain an asset register arranged in columns which include the following details— (a) date of purchase of asset; (b) location of asset; (c) name of supplier of the goods; (d) price paid (including VAT); (e) asset number (optional) which can be used as a unique identifier; (f) date when a check was conducted; (g) person responsible for checking; and (h) specific observations on the condition of assets. (3) The asset register shall be prepared and handled by the Director of Finance and Administration and the Bursar may assist in preparation of such register.

provisions.title: Institute 's furniture, equipment, vehicles and tools
provisions.content: (1) All major items of furniture and equipment for the Institute shall be purchased from Capital expenditure provided for that purpose. (2) To ensure proper account of all items, when they are issued from store and taken off stores ledger charge they shall be brought on charge in an inventory register or other record designed to suit the particular items concerned. (3) Furniture and equipment issued for use in offices shall be brought on charge in a master inventory, which shall record the date, and other details of the voucher or other document on which the items were received or issued, their serial numbers, where appropriate should be indicated and in the case of typewriters, calculators, their distributions to individuals locations and the total quantity hold must be shown. (4) The dates and other details of all movements of items changes in the location of items shall be rcorded as they occur. (5) The master inventory shall be kept at the Directorate of Finance and Administration. (6) The Personnel Officer shall keep records of all furniture and equipment issued to staff houses. (7) When the occupant of an office, quarter or location or other person having charge or use of the equipment or furniture changes, the items on charge shall be physically checked against the location of inventories by the officer maintaining the master inventory and copies signed by the outgoing officer and the incoming officer as evidence of his taking over the items, must be provided.

provisions.title: Purchase and custody of library books
provisions.content: (1) All books purchases for libraries or offices shall be brought on charge in catalogue or inventory and shall be given an accession number and retained in a suitable room or book case under lock and key. (2) Books may be issued on loan to officers requiring them for reference or study for official purposes. (3) A loan register shall be maintained shown the accession number and title of the books borrowed the name, designation and signature of the officer borrowing it, the date borrowed and returns date. (4) The period of loan shall not exceed one month. (5) Library books shall be checked at least once every quarter by an officer other than librarian. (6) Evidence of the check shall be recorded in the catalogue or inventory, signed and dated by the checking officer. Part IX – Contracts and consultancy

provisions.title: Contract records
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Finance and Administration and the Head of Legal Unit shall make sure that there is a register of contracts wherein the following information shall be provided:— (a) serial number of the contract; (b) name and address of the contractor; (c) date of the contract commencement and completion of contract; (d) reference to appropriate authority for the award of the contract; (e) brief description of the nature of contract; (f) cost of contract and details of its payment; (g) security retained and how eventually paid; and (h) remedy for breach of contract. (2) All payments relating to a contract shall be supported by a certificate of completion which shall contain the following information:— (a) number of certificate and reference to the contract; (b) date of payment and amount paid on that date; and (c) value of goods or services provided or work done analyzed into materials provided, amount paid in respect of wages, use of plant costs and other overhead costs as appropriate; and (d) security retained. (3) In case the contract is connected with construction, the supervising quantity surveyor or Architect shall countersign the authentification engaged by the Institute . (4) All contracts, offers and awards shall be made in accordance with the public Procurement Act and its subsequent Regulations, based on competitive and openness manner.

provisions.title: Research projects
provisions.content: (1) Where approaches are to be made to outside bodies for support for research project or where contracts are to be undertaken on behalf of such bodies, heads of department shall ensure that financial implications are appropriate and proper recognition of indirect costs have been fully discussed and appraised by the Management. (2) The Management shall be responsible for examining each formal grant application and ensure that adequate resources have been requested to meet all commitments. (3) The following information shall be required when examining each application:— (a) the financial value of the application including details of materials, equipment, travel and other direct and indirect costs; (b) name of sponsor; (c) period of award; and (d) payment terms and claiming arrangements.

provisions.title: Charges of services
provisions.content: (1) Particulars of all charges to be made for work done, service rendered or to be rendered or goods supplied or to be supplied and all other amounts due to the Institute shall be promptly notified to the Bursar for the purpose of raising charges or invoice to the party concerned. (2) The contract or agreement and financial proposal shall be attached to the invoice and copies of the agreement be kept by the Director of Finance and Administration and the Principal . (3) The members of staff concerned shall notify the Bursar of all moneys due to the Institute under agreements prepared in their section or cluster including contract agreements for sale of property and any agreement which involves the receipt of funds by the Institute and a copy of such agreement shall be forwarded to the Director of Finance and Administration and the Principal as early as possible. (4) Heads of department or division are required to take all proper steps to collect money from clients. (5) Official receipts shall be issued immediately as acknowledgement of sums received by authorized employees of the Institute , and when payment to the Institute is made by cheque, the details of such cheque shall be recorded properly. (6) All moneys received on behalf of the Institute , shall be paid to the accounts office and banked intact. (7) In case the money is deposited directly to the account, an official receipt will be issued to the client who deposited the funds after making confirmation with the bank. Part X – Miscellaneous provisions

provisions.title: Opening bank accounts
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall decide and approve all Institutes' banking Opening arrangements sought in order to open a new bank account or to close an old bank account. (2) All bank accounts shall be in the name of the Institute or one if its subsidiary organizations and shall be operated within the direction and control of the finance office and the Principal . (3) All bank transactions shall be recorded solely in the Institute 's records. (4) All cheques drawn on behalf of the Institute shall be signed in the form approved by the Bank signatories. (5) The Institute 's departments, units, divisions and sections are not allowed to have separate bank accounts.

provisions.title: Institute 's bank accounts
provisions.content: (1) The Institute shall arrange to open its bank accounts in one or institute's more banks in the Country, as it may deem necessary. (2) A bank account of the Institute shall not be used for any purpose other than official transaction nor shall it be overdrawn without proper approval of the Principal . (3) All cheques drawn by the Institute shall bear two signatures one from each of the following categories:— (a) Category "A" (i) Principal ; (ii) Director of Studies . (b) Caregory "B" (i) Director of Finance and Administration; (ii) Bursar .

provisions.title: Handing over
provisions.content: (1) Whenever any member of staff in a position of trust or who is entrusted with cash or other properties of the Institute goes on leave or is relieved from his duties, he shall hand over his work and any property of the Institute in his custody to another relevant member of staff . (2) A detailed report shall be prepared and signed by both the out-going and in-coming Officers, whereby copies of such report shall be submitted to the Principal and the Director of Finance and Administration who shall take action for settlement of any discrepancies found. (3) The officer handing over shall ensure that all cash books and registers are fully entered and balanced to the date of the handing over.

provisions.title: Motor vehicles registration and use
provisions.content: (1) All motor vehicles belonging to the Institute shall be registered in the name of the Institute and the Director of Finance and Administration shall insure that adequate records of vehicle registration, inspection and insurance are maintained. (2) The designated Transport Officer or Human Resource Officer shall ensure that motor vehicle are road worthy at all times, and that they comply with statutory regulations concerning carriage of goods and passengers. (3) A report upon any road accident in which the Institute 's vehicle is involved, however minor, shall be submitted promptly to the Director of Finance and Administration and shall be serviced as prescribed and its record for such service shall be maintained. (4) The logbooks for each Institute 's motor vehicle show details of fuel consumption, date refueled, and kilometers traveled and drivers shall be required to make appropriate entries in logbooks every time they make a trip. (5) The disignated Transport Officer or Human Resource Officer shall inspect all logbooks, at least once every week, and ensure that they are properly recorded, and where misuse is observed he shall immediately inform the Director of Finance and Administration who shall make appropriate investigation and take the necessary action. (6) The Institute ' vehicles shall not be used for private purposes, hired or lent out whether for payment or otherwise to any person or organization without the approval of the Principal .

provisions.title: Financial risk and taxation
provisions.content: (1) The Director of Finance and Administration shall be Financial responsible for preparing appropriate business continuity action plan on insurance protection for possible occurrence of major financial risks. (2) The Board shall receive an annual report on the Institute 's risk management issues and the Director of Finance and Administration shall be responssible for effecting insurance coverage. (3) It is the duty of the Bursar to advice the Management in all matters of taxation ralating to the Institute but further advice may be sought to the Director of Finance Administration.

provisions.title: Students' annual budget
provisions.content: (1) Students representatives shall approve Students' Association annual estimates budget and funds before forwarding it to the Institute 's Management. (2) The Students Association's annual financial statements shall be audited by external auditors appointed by the Institute in accordance with an agreement between the Institute and the Students's Association. (3) The Institute 's Internal Auditors shall have access to all records concerning the assets of Students’s Association. (4) Remunerations, allowances, equipment and facilities must be determined, from time to time, by the student's representatives via organs acceptable by their governing constitution.

provisions.title: Expansion of regulations
provisions.content: (1) The expansion of these regulations take into accounts the nature and functions of the Institute and shall govern all financial operations of the institute. (2) In case of any conflict or inadequacy of the provisions in these regulations, the provisions of the Public Finance Act and Public Procurement Act shall prevail.
